title: Inline owner-local `...Solved` forwarding wrappers in `MLF.Reify.Core`
problem: `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs` already presents `PresolutionView`-based reify entrypoints, but it still keeps five owner-local `Solved`-typed forwarding helpers (`reifyTypeWithNamesNoFallbackSolved`, `reifyTypeWithNamedSetSolved`, `reifyTypeWithNamedSetNoFallbackSolved`, `reifyBoundWithNamesSolved`, `reifyBoundWithNamesBoundSolved`). Each is only called once inside the same module, so they preserve an unnecessary internal `Solved`-flavored helper surface without providing a second supported API.
why_this_is_simpler: Keep `solvedFromView` local and unchanged, but inline those five single-use `...Solved` helper bodies back into the existing `PresolutionView` entrypoints. That removes dead internal wrapper names, keeps the owner-module seam explicit in one place, and leaves `reifyWith`/`reifyWithAs`/`freeVars` untouched so the cleanup stays narrowly structural.
why_thesis_exactness_should_be_preserved: This is a refactor of call structure, not reification semantics. Each affected entrypoint would still derive the same `Solved` from the same `PresolutionView`, use the same canonicalization and name lookup rules, pass the same `ReifyRoot` (`RootType`, `RootTypeNoFallback`, or `RootBound`), and end in the same `reifyWith` or `reifyWithAs` implementation. That preserves the thesis-facing Schi'/Schi_p behavior, binder-bound handling, and no-fallback quantifier logic exactly.
expected_benefits: Removes five dead internal `Solved` wrapper entrypoints from `MLF.Reify.Core`.
Makes the reify module easier to read by keeping only the real public `PresolutionView` API and the core `reifyWith` machinery.
Shrinks the remaining compatibility surface in the exact direction Task 65 asks for, without expanding scope into broader `Solved` or `PresolutionView` redesign.
risks: A copy-paste mistake could swap `RootType`, `RootTypeNoFallback`, or `RootBound` while inlining.
The duplicated `varNameFor` and `isNamed` logic must stay behaviorally identical after moving code.
Touching exported `Solved`-based helpers such as `reifyWithAs` or `freeVars` would over-expand the change and should be explicitly avoided.
required_validation: `rg -n "reifyTypeWithNamesNoFallbackSolved|reifyTypeWithNamedSetSolved|reifyTypeWithNamedSetNoFallbackSolved|reifyBoundWithNamesSolved|reifyBoundWithNamesBoundSolved" src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs` should return no matches after the cleanup.
`cabal test --test-show-details=direct --test-options='--match "chi-first guard: runtime and reify modules no longer adapt Solved through fromSolved"'`.
`test/PipelineSpec.hs:319` or `test/PipelineSpec.hs:474` should gain or tighten a source-shape guard that the removed `...Solved` helpers do not reappear in `src/MLF/Reify/Core.hs`.
